[2018-03-17 16:22] aeary. (Aeary): Hey, long time no see.
[2018-03-17 16:23] aeary. (Aeary): Zeze convinced me to pick this up after giving it a good review. Currently enjoying what little of it I've had the time to play.
[2018-03-17 16:23] gryph3155 (Gryph202): Que pasa, fellow swarmwielders?  😄
[2018-03-17 16:23] aeary. (Aeary): Hope you've been well.
[2018-03-17 16:23] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I have been, ty. That's awesome
[2018-03-17 16:23] gryph3155 (Gryph202): @Aeary , speaking only for myself, I lose big and lose often at Prismata, and I still love it.  😛
[2018-03-17 16:24] gryph3155 (Gryph202): In an industry full of Hearthstone ripoffs, Prismata and Gwent are badly needed shots in the arm.
[2018-03-17 16:25] aeary. (Aeary): I'm travelling atm so I've only had a few hours worth of play. Was working through the challenges earlier. Intend to finish them all before playing any players.
[2018-03-17 16:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea I did the same back when I started
[2018-03-17 16:25] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Have you played the campaign?
[2018-03-17 16:25] gryph3155 (Gryph202): That's a good start; you can also play casual matches with bots.
[2018-03-17 16:26] aeary. (Aeary): I was attracted by the promise of no RNG. Me and Zeze play chess together, so when he said those skills might be applicable here, I was happy to check it out.
[2018-03-17 16:26] Deleted User (Deleted User): i doubt you'll be able to take the extremely unfair challenge in tier 5 for a while
[2018-03-17 16:26] Deleted User (Deleted User): beating that is much harder than beating a fellow new player
[2018-03-17 16:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Why Yuji, Ash is an intelligent human :LongNoseLiar:
[2018-03-17 16:26] aeary. (Aeary): I was on tier 5 when I stopped
[2018-03-17 16:27] aeary. (Aeary): Zz said he'd give me an introductory talk but he wanted me to finish the tutorial first so I haven't taken him up on it yet.
[2018-03-17 16:27] gryph3155 (Gryph202): Technically, no RNG in terms of card draw, yomi, or such.  But each game is a unique experience when the common pool of variable units is put together.  😃
[2018-03-17 16:28] aeary. (Aeary): The unit variance between games is reminsincent of Legion TD to me. The mirrored aspect makes it far more fun to me, though.
[2018-03-17 16:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Oh man I love Legion TD
[2018-03-17 16:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I was actually playing it again for a few weeks some months ago
[2018-03-17 16:28] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But that's a topic for #video_games :D
[2018-03-17 16:29] gryph3155 (Gryph202): @Elyot wrote a blog post a while back, about how existing games (particularly Hearthstone) informed Prismata's design.  I should see if I can dig it up again.  It's a good read for new players.
[2018-03-17 16:30] aeary. (Aeary): I dislike games where game knowledge is an overly important element to success, while simultaneously having poor feedback loops.
[2018-03-17 16:30] gryph3155 (Gryph202): *But Gryph!  Prismata is nothing like Hearthstone!*  

Yup.  That's exactly the point.  😉
[2018-03-17 16:32] aeary. (Aeary): Ltd for me was like that. It was often unclear which decisions correlated to success and why, due to slight elements of randomness and precise numbers. It's much more observable in Prismata, which makes me interested in picking it up long term.
[2018-03-17 16:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Yea, I see what you mean
[2018-03-17 16:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): For learning resources, my favorite is this https://prismatalibrary.blog/archive/
Archive
Visit the post for more.
[2018-03-17 16:33] gryph3155 (Gryph202): Prismata is chock-full of "agency."  That is, every decision you make has consequences.
[2018-03-17 16:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): The articles are quite new. @307th started writing them not too long ago
[2018-03-17 16:34] aeary. (Aeary): I think until I feel like I'm at a competent enough level I don't want to refer to any external resources to learn. I feel I'd be better able to understand the game if I develop the logic and reasoning behind choice consequences myself. I can read them later to pick up on everything I've missed.
[2018-03-17 16:35] aeary. (Aeary): Zz told me the expert challenges were difficult, so I think once I've done those and all the combat training I'll feel a bit more content.
[2018-03-17 16:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Sounds good, gl hf ;P
[2018-03-17 16:36] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Time for me to resume practice, I have a tourney match on Monday (Prismata Golden Cup)

[2018-03-17 16:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): There's a custom rules tourney happening soon
[2018-03-17 16:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): The Wheel by @Redrame

[2018-03-18 02:23] telna: I'm struggling to understand the use for the alt drones
[2018-03-18 02:23] telna: especially like people getting doomed drone early?
[2018-03-18 02:23] telna: isn't that like the opposite of when you want to be going short-term?
[2018-03-18 02:24] f300xen (f0x): you get it so you can get tech buildings first
[2018-03-18 02:24] f300xen (f0x): so you can develop attackers or something
[2018-03-18 02:25] telna: a way to rush I guess?
[2018-03-18 02:26] iminabearsuit: Doomed drone is the best unit in the game by far.
[2018-03-18 02:26] f300xen (f0x): imo drone is better than doomed drone
[2018-03-18 02:27] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Didn't check the maths, but for example if buying a normal Drone would result in floating 1 gold and 1 Energy, then I'd instead buy 2 Doomed Drones

[2018-03-18 02:29] iminabearsuit: You don't generally open with them, unless to get an early tech option. But the goal of most midecon games with doomed drone , should be to exhaust the supply of them.
[2018-03-18 02:31] supergreyman (Greyman): Doomed drone is like Engineer but can give you a net +1 gold instead of -2 gold.
[2018-03-18 02:34] supergreyman (Greyman): Or lets you go for a specific tech or unit much faster without slowing down your econ (for example, p2 can go 1. DD, 2. DB+Doomed Drone).
